mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >55% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to yield positive forward returns in >50% of observed cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output, generating positive short-term momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative forward returns in >65% of cases during recovery phases, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index year-over-year growth exceeds 4.0%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPI YoY exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive returns in >50% of cases over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, systematically generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth rate shows no statistically significant correlation with forward returns or yields negative returns in >60% of expanding quarters.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate above 5.0% and a consumer price index above 300 signals a restrictive monetary stance amid persistent inflation, triggering stagflation concerns and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint high-rate and high-inflation regime produces positive forward returns in >55% of instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
